alert-panel.preview:
Power Outage — Your Area is Affected

April 28, 2026 9:15 AM

We are aware of a power outage affecting customers in your area. Our crews have been dispatched and are working to restore service as quickly and safely as possible.

Estimated restoration time: Today by 6:00 PM

Customers affected: Approximately 1,240 in the 90210 area

Cause: Equipment failure on a transmission line near Wilshire Blvd and Fairfax Ave.

Outage Update — Crews On Site, Investigating Cause

April 28, 2026 10:45 AM

SCE crews have arrived on site and are actively investigating the cause of the outage in your area.

Current status: Crews have isolated the affected section of the grid and are assessing damage to a distribution transformer on Elm St near 5th Ave, Pasadena.

Revised estimated restoration: Today by 2:30 PM.

Power Restored — Outage in Your Area Has Been Resolved

April 27, 2026 1:52 PM

Power has been fully restored to all customers affected by today's outage. We apologize for the disruption.

Public Safety Power Shutoff (PSPS) Watch — High Wind Event

April 26, 2026 6:30 AM

Due to forecasted high winds and low humidity, your area is under a PSPS watch and power may be proactively shut off to prevent wildfires.

Watch window: April 27 at 8:00 PM through April 28 at 6:00 AM

Planned Outage — Infrastructure Upgrade in Your Neighborhood

April 24, 2026 3:20 PM

SCE will conduct a planned outage in your neighborhood to replace aging infrastructure and improve long-term service reliability.

Scheduled outage window: Thursday, May 1, 2026 from 8:00 AM to 12:00 PM

Platform Behavior
How the Alert Panel adapts between desktop and mobile. Engineers should implement these differences via a single media query breakpoint at max-width: 768px rather than separate components.
Property Desktop Mobile
Width Constrained to content container. Follows page grid — typically 600–800px max-width. Full viewport width, edge-to-edge. border-radius: 0 on mobile.
Alert Title 16px / 700 Bold 14px / 700 Bold ↓ 2px
Alert Body 14px / 400 Regular 13px / 400 Regular ↓ 1px
Timestamp / Meta 12px / 400 — unchanged on mobile 12px / 400 — same ✓
Dismiss Button 24px icon. No enlarged tap zone needed — mouse cursor is precise. 24px icon inside a 44×44px tap zone (padding or pseudo-element). Required for WCAG 2.5.5.
Action Button Inline, right-aligned next to the alert body text. Full-width, stacked below the body text. Avoids cramped inline layout on narrow screens.
Message Strip / Toast Floats — positioned fixed; bottom: 24px; right: 24px. Auto-width. Anchored — fixed; bottom: 0; left: 0; width: 100%. Full-width, flush to bottom.
Visual Comparison
Desktop

April 28, 2026 9:15 AM

We are aware of a power outage affecting customers in your area. Our crews have been dispatched and are working to restore service.

← inline action

title: 16px/700 · body: 14px · dismiss: 16px icon · action: inline
Mobile

Power Outage — Your Area is Affected

44×44

April 28, 2026 9:15 AM

We are aware of a power outage affecting customers in your area. Our crews have been dispatched and are working to restore service.

↑ full-width action below body
title: 14px/700 · body: 13px · dismiss: 44×44 tap zone · action: full-width
CSS Reference — Engineering
/* Alert Panel — base (desktop) */
.alert-card__title { font-size: 16px; font-weight: 700; }
.alert-card__body { font-size: 14px; }
.alert-card__action { display: inline; }
.alert-card__dismiss { width: 24px; height: 24px; }
.alert-card { border-radius: 8px; }

/* Alert Panel — mobile overrides */
@media (max-width: 768px) {
.alert-card__title { font-size: 14px; } /* ↓ 2px */
.alert-card__body { font-size: 13px; } /* ↓ 1px */
.alert-card { border-radius: 0; width: 100%; } /* edge-to-edge */
.alert-card__dismiss { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; } /* WCAG 2.5.5 */
.alert-card__action { display: block; width: 100%; margin-top: 12px; }
.message-strip { bottom: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; border-radius: 0; }
}

From routine maintenance to emergency response, our field teams keep Southern California powered 24/7.

codeFiles.name: C24bCard.tsx
Usage.tsx
codeFiles.source:
// Usage — C24bCard
import { C24bCard } from './C24bCard';
import { Application } from '@carbon/icons-react';

// 1-column desktop
<C24bCard
  src="/images/crew-maintenance.jpg"
  icon={<Application size={20} />}
  title="Our Crews Work Around the Clock for You"
  description="From routine maintenance to emergency response, our field teams keep Southern California powered 24/7."
  size="1col"
  href="/reliability"
/>

// 2-column desktop
<C24bCard
  src="/images/solar-panels.jpg"
  icon={<Application size={20} />}
  title="Go Solar with SCE Incentives"
  description="SCE's solar incentive programs make switching to clean energy more affordable."
  size="2col"
  href="/solar"
/>

// 3-column desktop
<C24bCard
  src="/images/energy-home.jpg"
  icon={<Application size={20} />}
  title="Energy-Saving Tips"
  description="Small changes at home can lower your monthly bill."
  size="3col"
  href="/tips"
/>

// Mobile (no photo)
<C24bCard
  icon={<Application size={18} />}
  title="Manage Your Account On the Go"
  description="Pay your bill, track usage, and report outages anytime with the SCE app."
  size="mobile"
  href="/app"
/>

  src="/images/sunset-grid.jpg"

/>

  src="/images/solar-panels.jpg"

/>

// Mobile

  src="/images/outage-crews.jpg"
  title="Manage outages on the go."
  size="mobile"
  href="/outages"
/>

alert-variants.preview:
All Variants
The five semantic alert types available in the SCE Design System: Error, Inform, Warn, Success, and Pewter (neutral/system).

Error: Action Required

April 28, 2026 9:15 AM

We were unable to process your payment. Please verify your billing information and try again.

Your April Statement is Ready

April 28, 2026 8:00 AM

Your bill for April 2026 is now available. Total due: $134.20. Due date: May 15, 2026.

Scheduled Maintenance — May 3, 2026

April 26, 2026 6:30 AM

Power will be temporarily interrupted in your area on May 3 from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM for infrastructure upgrades.

Payment Received

April 27, 2026 1:52 PM

Your payment of $134.20 has been processed successfully. A confirmation has been sent to your email on file.

System Notice

April 24, 2026 3:20 PM

Our online portal will be undergoing maintenance Saturday, May 4 from 2:00 AM to 5:00 AM.

codeFiles.name: Alert.tsx
Usage.tsx
codeFiles.source:
// Usage — All Variants
import { Alert } from "./components/alerts";

<Alert type="error"   title="Error"   message="..." />
<Alert type="inform"  title="Inform"  message="..." />
<Alert type="success" title="Success" message="..." />
<Alert type="warn"    title="Warn"    message="..." />
<Alert type="pewter"  title="Pewter"  message="..." />

alert-error.preview:
Error
Used for critical failures, blocked states, or when immediate user action is required. Rendered with an assertive ARIA live region.

Error: Action Required

April 28, 2026 9:15 AM

We were unable to process your payment. Please verify your billing information and try again, or contact us at 1-800-655-4555.

Usage.tsx
codeFiles.source:
// Usage — Error
import { Alert } from "./components/alerts";

<Alert
  type="error"
  title="Error — Action Required"
  message="There was a problem processing your request. Please try again."
/>

alert-inform.preview:
Inform
Used for informational updates that don't require immediate action. Rendered with a polite ARIA live region.

Your April Statement is Ready

April 28, 2026 8:00 AM

Your bill for April 2026 is now available. Total due: $134.20. Due date: May 15, 2026.

  title="Payment Confirmed"

alert-warn.preview:
Warn
Flags an important condition that may need attention but doesn't block the user. Also used for PSPS watches, session timeouts, and service advisories.

Scheduled Maintenance — May 3, 2026

April 26, 2026 6:30 AM

Power will be temporarily interrupted in your area on May 3 from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM for infrastructure upgrades.

alert-pewter.preview:
Pewter
Neutral / system notices. Used for low-priority informational messages, maintenance windows, or beta feature announcements that don't require urgent attention.

System Notice

April 24, 2026 3:20 PM

Our online portal will be undergoing maintenance Saturday, May 4 from 2:00 AM to 5:00 AM. Some features may be temporarily unavailable.

  message="Generic notification for non-severity messages."
/>

alert-with-links.preview:
With Links
Alert variant with contextual navigation links and an optional home/back button. Typically used for error states that redirect the user, such as 404 pages or session expiry.

Page Not Found

The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved. Here are some helpful links:

Start service Pay my bill Report an outage

alert-message-strip.preview:
Message Strip
A compact inline stack of dismissible notifications. Used in dashboard headers or contextual areas where space is limited and multiple alerts need to be surfaced together.

btn-sizes.preview:
Sizes
Four sizes with platform-specific guidance. Small is desktop-only — it falls below the 44px touch target minimum. Medium and above are safe on all platforms. Use Full Width on mobile for primary actions.
Desktop
All four sizes available. Small is permitted in dense toolbars and compact data layouts where touch input is not expected.
Mobile
Small is retired. Minimum height is 44px (Medium) to meet Apple HIG & WCAG touch target requirements. Prefer Full Width for primary actions.
Desktop
Primary
SM · 36px Desktop only
MD · 44px Default
LG · 52px High-emphasis
XL · 60px Hero CTA
Secondary
SM · 36px Desktop only
MD · 44px Default
LG · 52px High-emphasis
XL · 60px Hero CTA
Mobile
Inline (Primary)
36px
✕
SM · Retired Too small
MD · 44px ✓ Touch safe
LG · 52px ✓ Touch safe
Full Width — preferred for primary mobile actions

btn-states.preview:
States
Each variant has Default, Hover, and Disabled states. Disabled buttons use #D5D5D5 fill and #A5B7BA text; they are non-interactive and should not receive focus.

btn-cta-arrow.preview:
CTA Arrow
Inline call-to-action link with a trailing arrow icon. Use for contextual navigation links inside cards, alerts, or body copy — not as a primary action button. Accepts a custom color to work on light and dark surfaces.
Default (Blue)
On Dark Background
